items.title: AI Oligarchs Are Driving Without Insurance
items.summary: OpenAI operates machines that introduce far greater systemic risk than any vehicle, yet they are entirely exempt from liability insurance.

items.title: The Mechanics of Effortless Action vs. Calculated Compliance
items.summary: An examination of why manipulative communication frameworks trigger biological disgust, and how effortless action (wu-wei) aligns with the physics of human information exchange.

items.title: Egregores and the Magosphere
items.summary: Terry Mancour's magosphere reframes egregores as observable systems — belief as measurable force interacting with a reactive environment to produce contingent consciousness.

items.title: The Call Center Workers Should Be Training AI on Emotions
items.summary: Millions of people who spent years reading strangers' emotional states through voice alone are about to lose their jobs. They're also the most calibrated emotional sensors on the planet.

items.title: How Footprints in Mud Exposed the Colonial Origins of Archeology
items.summary: Human footprints at White Sands proved people lived in the Americas 23,000 years ago — 10,000 years before archeologists said was possible, exactly when Indigenous people said it was.

items.title: Iblings: The Reproductive Nature of Human-AI Interaction
items.summary: Human-AI interaction is functionally reproductive. Two pattern systems combine inputs. Something emerges that neither could produce alone.

items.title: The Artificial Scarcity of Truth: How "The Vibe" Became the New Censorship
items.summary: When Hasan Minhaj silenced Neil deGrasse Tyson for being 'boring,' he demonstrated how modern society systematically erases expertise through social pressure.

items.title: Cutting Stones and Decolonizing My Manager Brain
items.summary: The Three Stone Cutters parable isn't a gift of purpose — it's a mechanism to colonize someone's mind into giving you their soul when you're only paying for their time.

items.title: The Collaborator That Acts or Asks
items.summary: Most tools wait for instructions. The useful ones follow a protocol: attempt everything autonomously, stop only at genuine structural barriers, escalate with surgical precision, resume from exactly where they stopped.
